## Summary
Dark matter halo density profiles are well-described by the NFW profile, with concentrations that follow a mass-concentration relation. Satellite kinematics, weak lensing, and X-ray observations constrain halo structure across five decades in mass.

## Evidence
N-body simulations consistently produce NFW-like profiles with inner slope ~r⁻¹. Observational constraints from stacked weak lensing (DES, HSC) confirm the predicted concentration-mass relation within 10%. Deviations at the core suggest possible self-interacting dark matter effects.

## Significance
Halo profiles are a cornerstone of galaxy formation models and cosmological inference. Deviations from CDM predictions at small scales could reveal dark matter particle properties.
